nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving streams, tributaries, and floodplain habitats of the Rio Paraguay, Rio Paraná, and Rio Uruguay drainages; areas shaded by floating or overhanging vegetation, with leaf litter and submerged roots; subject to significant seasonal variation in water parameters

decorPreferences: dense planting with fine-leaved plants (Java moss, hornwort, water sprite)

diet.notes: Unselective micro-predator that accepts most aquarium foods. High-quality flake, micro-pellets, and granules form a solid staple diet. Live and frozen foods (brine shrimp, daphnia, bloodworms, tubifex, mosquito larvae) should be offered regularly to bring out best coloration and condition. Feed small portions 2–3 times daily.

goodWith: small peaceful tetras (ember tetra, neon tetra, cardinal tetra)
avoidWith: long-finned varieties (fancy guppies, bettas, angelfish) — fin-nipping risk
very small nano fish that may be intimidated

imageCandidates.notes: FishBase image for Aphyocharax rathbuni; verify availability and license before use.

summary: Rathbun’s Tetra is a small, active South American schooling fish reaching 4–5 cm, prized for its emerald-green body and vivid red flush along the lower flank. Hardy and cool-tolerant (to 18°C), it is well suited to unheated subtropical setups and community tanks. Keep in groups of 6 or more.
careNotes: Rathbun’s Tetra (trade name for the fish widely catalogued under the valid name Aphyocharax rathbuni Eigenmann, 1907) is endemic to the Rio Paraguay, Rio Paraná, and Rio Uruguay drainages across Paraguay, Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay. It is frequently confused with related Hyphessobrycon species in the trade due to superficial color similarities and overlapping common names; the genus Aphyocharax (subfamily Aphyocharacinae) is distinct from Hyphessobrycon (subfamily Stevardiinae / Tetragonopterinae). A minimum group of six is essential — solitary or paired specimens become stressed and display poor coloration. The species is notably cool-tolerant, thriving down to 18°C, making it one of the few tetras suitable for subtropical unheated aquariums in temperate climates. A planted aquarium with dark substrate and floating plants to diffuse overhead light brings out the iridescent green and red coloration to best effect. Although generally peaceful, Rathbun’s Tetra has a reputation for fin-nipping, particularly toward long-finned or slow-moving tankmates; robust, similarly sized community fish are preferable. Water quality should be maintained with regular partial water changes; the species is highly adaptable to a broad range of water chemistry within the stated parameters. Colors in juveniles are subdued — full adult coloration (males: white fin tips on dorsal, anal, and pelvic fins; females: fuller body) develops over approximately 16 months.
breedingNotes: Egg scatterers that spawn readily in groups given appropriate conditions. A separate 40–60 L breeding tank with fine-leaved plants (Java moss, hornwort), a spawning mop, or sterilized marbles over the base will protect eggs from being eaten. Slightly soft water (gH 2–8), pH 6.5, and a gentle temperature increase to around 25–26°C triggers spawning, which typically occurs at dawn. The dominant male and largest female account for most of the egg deposition; up to 500 eggs may be produced per spawn. Remove adults immediately after spawning to prevent egg predation. Eggs hatch in 2–3 days; fry are free-swimming within 4–6 days. Feed fry with infusoria or commercial fry food initially, progressing to freshly hatched brine shrimp nauplii. Brood difficulty is rated easy; no specialized conditioning is required beyond a clean, well-planted setup.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Aphyocharax rathbuni
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided native range (Paraguay, Paraná, Uruguay drainages; Brazil, Paraguay, Argentina, Uruguay), temperature 20–26°C, pH 6.5–7.5, hardness 36–357 ppm, adult size 35–45 mm SL, habitat description, behavior (fin-nipper, schooling), diet, and breeding notes (egg scatterer, up to 500 eggs, hatch 2–3 days).

additional.name: FishBase — Aphyocharax rathbuni
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Characidae, Aphyocharacinae, Characiformes), maximum size (7.1 cm SL morphological max; aquarium specimens typically 4–5 cm), native range (Paraguay River basin), IUCN Least Concern status, and named after Dr. Richard Rathbun (1852–1918).

additional.name: AquaInfo.nl — Aphyocharax rathbuni Green Fire Tetra
additional.notes: Key source for cool-tolerance lower bound of 18°C; confirmed breeding temperature 25.5°C, synonyms (Aphyocharax stramineus, Piabarchus stramineus), GH 8–12, native range includes Rio Paraguay and Rio Guaporé, schooling group of ~12 recommended for best display.

additional.name: Fishkeeper.co.uk — Rathbun’s Bloodfin Tetra
additional.notes: Confirmed native range (tributaries of Rio Paraguay in Paraguay), water parameters (20–26°C, pH 6.0–7.0, dH up to 18), adult size 4 cm, peaceful temperament, fin-nipping risk with long-finned fish, omnivorous diet, and egg-scattering breeding behavior.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Green Fire Tetra
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size 4–5 cm, minimum tank 60 L, temperature 20–26°C, pH 6–7.5, hardness 8–20°N, schooling behavior, omnivorous diet, egg-scattering breeding requiring fine-leaved plants, lifespan 3–6 years.

fish.breedingNotes: Egg scatterers; group spawning in planted tank with fine-leaved plants or spawning mop. Remove adults after spawning. Up to 500 eggs; hatch in 2–3 days; fry free-swimming in 4–6 days. Begin feeding with infusoria, then brine shrimp nauplii.
